rule_metadata.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
rule_metadata.title: Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons

rules.rule_text:
title: Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons
id: 9eb99343-d336-4020-a3cd-67f3819e68ee
status: test
description: This method uses uncommon error codes on failed logons to determine suspicious
  activity and tampering with accounts that have been disabled or somehow restricted.
references:
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/it-pro/windows-10/security/threat-protection/auditing/event-4625
- https://twitter.com/SBousseaden/status/1101431884540710913
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
date: 2017-02-19
modified: 2025-10-17
tags:
- attack.persistence
- attack.defense-evasion
- attack.privilege-escalation
- attack.initial-access
- attack.t1078
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection_eid:
    EventID:
    - 4625
    - 4776
  selection_status:
  - Status:
    - '0xC0000072'
    - '0xC000006F'
    - '0xC0000070'
    - '0xC0000413'
    - '0xC000018C'
    - '0xC000015B'
  - SubStatus:
    - '0xC0000072'
    - '0xC000006F'
    - '0xC0000070'
    - '0xC0000413'
    - '0xC000018C'
    - '0xC000015B'
  filter:
    SubjectUserSid: S-1-0-0
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter
falsepositives:
- User using a disabled account
level: medium
